text: Handling deadlines effectively is an important skill in both work and study. Good time management helps you break large tasks into smaller, manageable steps. Start by listing everything you need to do, then prioritise the most urgent or important tasks. Scope management is also crucial—if a project starts growing beyond its original plan, you may need to adjust your expectations. Finally, communication is key. If you realise you might miss a deadline, let the relevant people know early. Explaining your situation and proposing a solution is much better than staying silent. By combining these strategies, you can reduce stress and meet deadlines more confidently.
